news.headline: Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (NYSE: EPD) offers 2023 Schedule K-3 forms online for international tax matters. Investors can access at www.taxpackagesupport.com/enterprise.
news.summary: Houston-based Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (NYSE: EPD) disclosed the availability of its 2023 Schedule K-3 forms, containing pertinent international tax details. Interested unitholders, including foreign investors, those claiming a foreign tax credit, and select corporate or partnership unitholders, can view the information at www.taxpackagesupport.com/enterprise. This allows them to access crucial tax-related data online.

news.headline: Port of Houston Authority engages 2000 community members in outreach activities, fostering community stewardship.
news.summary: In Houston, the Port Commission of the Port of Houston Authority convened for its June monthly meeting, chaired by Port Commissioner Dean Corgey. During the meeting, it was reported that significant community outreach efforts had engaged almost 2,000 residents of neighboring areas by the end of May. This outreach involved various forums like community events, workshops, and meetings. The Port Commission prioritizes community engagement and appreciates the active involvement of around 2,000 individuals from nearby communities in these activities.

The market is expected to have a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.78% during this period. This growth is attributed to the rising prevalence of...

news.headline: Global 3D printing in education sector market to grow by USD 1.10 billion from 2024-2028, with a CAGR of 13.64%.
news.summary: According to Technavio, the global market size for 3D printing in the education sector is projected to increase by USD 1.10 billion between 2024 and 2028. The market is expected to experience a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of nearly 13.64% throughout the forecast period. The growth is attributed to the advantages provided by 3D printing technology in education.

news.headline: Ocean Power Technologies partners with Teledyne Marine to enhance AI sensor capabilities and drive innovation in marine tech.
news.summary: OPT has entered into an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) agreement with Teledyne Marine to broaden its AI sensor capabilities and foster innovation in marine technology. This move aims to improve product offerings and stimulate growth for both companies.

news.summary: In Toronto on June 27, 2024, the Canadian Apartment Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (CAR.UN on TSX) disclosed its plan to release financial results for the three and six months concluding on June 30, 2024, after the market closes.

news.headline: ERES REIT to announce Q2 2024 results on June 30 after markets close.
news.summary: European Residential Real Estate Investment Trust, also known as ERES, disclosed on June 27, 2024, that it will be releasing its financial results for the second quarter and first half of the year on June 30, 2024, after the market closes. The company's performance in these periods will be made public following the closing of the market. ERES, listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol ERE.UN, will provide detailed figures and insights into its financial status during this time frame. Investors and interested individuals can anticipate the disclosure of important data and key metrics related to the real estate investment trust's operations and financial health.

news.headline: Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) declares $3.15/share third quarter 2024 dividend payable on Sept 27 to holders as of Sept 3.
news.summary: In Bethesda, Maryland, on June 27, 2024, the board of directors of Lockheed Martin Corporation (NYSE: LMT) approved a third quarter 2024 dividend of $3.15 per share. This dividend will be distributed on September 27, 2024, to shareholders of record as of the end of business on September 3, 2024.

news.headline: WildBrain (TSX: WILD) nearing completion of refinancing existing credit facilities, to close soon.
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, WildBrain Ltd., a prominent company in kids' and family entertainment (TSX: WILD), shared that they are in the final stages of refinancing their current credit facilities. The refinancing process is anticipated to conclude soon.

news.headline: Yann Le Pallec appointed as President of S&P Global Ratings, previously Executive Managing Director.
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, S&P Global Ratings revealed the promotion of Yann Le Pallec to President. Formerly the Executive Managing Director and Head of Global Ratings Services, Le Pallec will now lead the company. This advancement marks a significant development in S&P Global Ratings, a notable player in the financial world.

news.headline: Brookfield Reinsurance to hold virtual shareholders meeting on July 22, 2024 at 10:30 a.m. (EDT).

news.headline: Sachem Capital Corp. retracts USD-denominated debt offering due to unfavorable market pricing conditions.
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, Sachem Capital Corp. (NYSE American: SACH) revealed the cancellation of its planned debt offering. Initially set to introduce USD-denominated unsecured, unsubordinated notes maturing in five years, the company opted out due to unfavorable market conditions. Sachem Capital Corp. deemed the pricing constraints as excessive and disadvantageous for both the company and its shareholders.

news.headline: GCHI's Harleyville plants win top environmental award at 2024 PCA Fly-in, recognized for outstanding performance.

(GCHI) revealed that its local plants, Giant Cement Company (GCC) and Giant Resource Recovery, Inc. (GRR!), received prestigious recognition for their exceptional Environmental Performance. This recognition highlights the commitment of the company's plants in Harleyville in maintaining high environmental standards. It is a significant achievement for GCHI, showcasing their dedication to sustainability and eco-friendly practices.

news.headline: BKV sells Carbon Sequestered Gas product to Kiewit, marking significant milestone in environmental innovation.
news.summary: In a recent announcement on June 27, 2024, BKV Corporation (BKV) revealed a significant deal they inked with Kiewit Infrastructure South Co., a subsidiary of Kiewit Corporation, a renowned entity in North America. The contract involves the sale and acquisition of Carbon Sequestered Gas (CSG), marking a promising development in the energy sector. This collaboration has garnered attention as BKV secures its position in the market, showcasing their commitment to innovative solutions for enhancing sustainability. The deal aims to leverage the expertise of these two industry giants to drive progress in eco-friendly practices.

news.headline: Tenaya Therapeutics promotes Kathy Ivey, Ph.D., to Senior VP, Research; Timothy Hoey, Ph.D., transitions to advisory role.
news.summary: Kathy Ivey, who holds a Ph.D., has been promoted to the position of Senior Vice President of Research within the organization. The company's recent announcement also highlighted that Timothy Hoey, Ph.D., will be transitioning to an advisory role. This change in leadership marks a strategic shift in the organization.

news.headline: Lisa Warren, DO '01, MBA named Dean of COMP & COMP-Northwest by WesternU; Effective July 1, 2024.

Warren is set to assume her new role starting July 1, 2024. This decision marks a significant development for the institution and highlights their confidence in Warren's ability to lead the medical colleges.

news.headline: FLINT will pay $5,205,194 in Senior Secured Debentures to Canso for interest due, per amended trust indenture.

(“FLINT”) (TSX: FLNT) announced that Canso Investment Counsel Ltd., as the portfolio manager for certain accounts, has agreed to accept $5,205,194 worth of Senior Secured Debentures on June 30, 2024 to cover the upcoming interest payment due on October 14, 2027. This "Payment in Kind Transaction" is part of the discretion allowed to Canso under the terms of the amended trust indenture governing the Senior Secured Debentures.

news.summary: Health experts advising the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention are recommending that Americans make a point of getting vaccinated annually as the virus undergoes mutations.

news.headline: Health care support workers in Newfoundland and Labrador are struggling with low pay, resulting in recruitment and retention issues. Over 1,400 signatures have been collected for a petition urging the government to address these concerns.
news.summary: In St. John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador, support and service staff working for Newfoundland and Labrador Health Services and represented by the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) have collected more than 1,400 signatures on a petition urging the Furey government to address recruitment and retention problems. Melissa Osmond, a housekeeper at Corne, expressed concerns about inadequate pay leading to staff attrition, stating, "We are not compensated fairly, prompting employees to seek better-paying opportunities elsewhere."

news.headline: Stay cool in downtown Plano, Texas this summer with 5 fun activities. Beat the heat for an exciting experience.
news.summary: In Plano, Texas, as of June 27, 2024, despite the soaring temperatures, residents are encouraged to delve into the summer activities without breaking a sweat. The downtown area of Plano offers a refreshing escape from the heat, providing numerous ways to stay cool and make the most of the season. A popular spot to beat the heat is the Urban Crust Icehouse Bar, where visitors can chill out and enjoy a cool atmosphere while having a great time.

news.headline: Inflation report set markets up for record highs in June, PCE index expected to decrease to 2.6% in May. Bullish sentiments may return on favorable data. 5 ETFs to watch for potential impact.
news.summary: The Personal Consumption Expenditure (PCE) price index, closely monitored by the Federal Reserve, was released for April data on May 31, leading to a market rally in June with major indexes hitting all-time highs. The upcoming PCE price index report for May, to be released at 8:30 a.m. ET, is highly anticipated by traders as they wonder if positive inflation data could reignite bullish sentiment on Wall Street. Economists expect the PCE price index to decrease from 2.7% year-on-year in April to 2.6% in May, with a monthly rise of 0.1%. The core PCE price index is projected to drop from 2.8% to 2.6% year-on-year in May, with a monthly increase of 0.1%. If PCE inflation falls as expected, it could boost investor expectations of rate cuts.

news.headline: Dow Jones drops 299 points as Fed official comments on interest rates, Nvidia rises after Wall Street call.
news.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average plunged by 299 points amidst growth in other market indexes. A prominent Federal Reserve official made remarks on interest rates. Nvidia experienced a significant rally in today's stock market session.

news.headline: Metro West Chapter of Credit Unions in Michigan offers grants up to $2,500 for non-profits to support local community activities.
news.summary: The Metro West Chapter of Credit Unions in Michigan is currently offering grants of up to $2,500 for non-profit organizations in Livonia. SC rejects plan to shield Sacklers in Purdue Pharma deal, jeopardizing it. Family members face civil claims related to opioid crisis.
news.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a bankruptcy deal that aimed to shield Sackler family members from liability in opioid crisis lawsuits. The maneuver, if accepted, would have safeguarded family members from civil claims stemming from the epidemic. The court's decision has significant implications as it directly impacts the Sackler family's ability to avoid facing legal consequences related to the crisis.

news.summary: The upcoming presidential debate on Thursday, hosted by CNN, has the potential to bring Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies to the forefront of political discussion, potentially reaching a broad audience.

news.headline: Live Oak Bancshares names Michael J. Cairns as Chief Credit Officer on Aug 1, 2024, succeeding Steven J. Smits who served for 9 years. Smits moves to head commercial banking.

Cairns will hold this position for Live Oak Bancshares and Live Oak Bank, taking over from Steven J. Smits, who held the role for 9 years. Smits will transition to a new position as head of commercial banking.

news.headline: Aruza Pest Control acquires Evergreen Pest Solutions operations in Charlotte; focuses on pest, termite, and mosquito control in multiple states.
news.summary: A pest control company called Aruza Pest Control Holdings, based in Charlotte, N.C, announced on June 27, 2024, that it specializes in providing pest, termite, and mosquito control services for residential and commercial properties. Aruza operates primarily in North and South Carolina, Florida, and Texas. It is regarded as a top player in the industry. The company is owned by Concentric Equity, and it serves a significant number of customers across its operational states.

It is expected to maintain a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 30.08% throughout the forecast period. This growth is attributed to the expanding electric vehicle automotive sector...

news.headline: S&P Global appoints Martina L. Cheung as President & CEO, succeeding Douglas L. Peterson who will retire on Nov 1, 2024. Peterson to be a special advisor until Dec 31, 2025. NY, June 27, 2024. NYSE: SPGI.
news.summary: Martina L. Cheung has been named the new President and Chief Executive Officer of the company, while current CEO Douglas L. Peterson will retire. The transition in leadership is set to take place on November 1, 2024. Mr. Peterson will continue to serve as a special advisor to the company until December 31, 2025. The announcement was made on June 27, 2024, in New York. S&P Global, listed as SPGI on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), shared this news.

news.headline: Nike stock dropped by X% after executives cited difficulties with demand, impacting company performance.
JPMorgan analyst reacts to US bank stress test results; significant surprise with increased stress capital buffers for large-cap banks. Capital requirements likely to delay future investment plans.
news.summary: A banking industry expert at JPMorgan has shared insights on the recent announcement of the Federal Reserve’s stress test results, which evaluate the financial stability of U.S. banks through analyzing potential losses, revenues, expenses, and capital levels in different economic scenarios. The results were disclosed on Wednesday.Analyst Vivek Juneja summarized the stress test outcomes for major banks, remarking that they were more surprising than anticipated. Most large-cap banks saw a significant rise in their stress capital buffers (SCB), consequently leading to an increase in their capital requirements. This notable growth in capital requirements is likely to affect the timing or extent of long-term capital return strategies.Juneja noted that all banks assessed surpassed their minimum necessities and are poised to gain from further accumulation of OCI (Other Comprehensive Income) losses. Nevertheless, it is expected that money center banks in the sector will experience delays or revisions in their capital return plans due to the augmented capital requirements.For more detailed information, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Fly-E Group, Inc. completes over-allotment option selling 337,500 extra shares at $4 each, totaling $10.35M gross proceeds.
news.summary: New York, N.Y., June 27, 2024 - Fly-E Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: FLYE) shared that the underwriter for its initial public offering exercised its over-allotment option fully, acquiring an extra 337,500 shares of FLYE's common stock at US$4.00 each. Including this option, FLYE has now sold a total of 2,587,500 shares at US$10.35 million in gross proceeds, prior to deductions for underwriter discounts, commissions, and related expenses. The company's common stock began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “FLYE” on June 6, 2024.

news.headline: Sonsray's Force for Good Initiative promotes community involvement by allowing employees a day off to volunteer.
news.summary: Located in Torrance, California, Sonsray demonstrates its commitment to people and the community through the Force for Good program. This initiative allows employees to dedicate a day to volunteering with a charity of their preference.

news.headline: BrightView Holdings increases receivables financing facility from $275M to $325M, extending maturity to June 2027.
news.summary: In Blue Bell, Pennsylvania, BrightView Holdings, Inc. revealed that its subsidiary, BrightView Funding LLC, has augmented its receivables financing agreement from $275 million to $325 million. This extension lasts till June 2027, offering the company a flexible and cost-effective debt option. The Receivables Facility also encompasses an "accordion feature" for further expansion.
